notes: SHIP v1.14.254 — reh3d FLAT|3D TOGGLE REWIRED: the .236 retirement is REVERSED (John's ruling, standing since 2026-07-12; executed per HANDOFF-reh3d-rewire-REV2.md, which the OPEN BOARD calls 'the original mission'). This handoff was ORPHANED ONCE — its original .244 stamp was consumed by MONOLITH PLATE — so it is now landed and recorded. THREE EDITS, all inside the rd branch of the rack-hybrid render. NO CSS (all .reh-3d-* rules were already resident), NO new function, NO scene-internals change. WHY THE .236 REASONING NO LONGER HOLDS, argued rather than asserted: .236 retired the toggle on 'one door per feature' + 'two 3D views compete for the single GL context'. (a) THE SOLO-RACK 3D INSPECT VIEW IS A DISTINCT SURFACE FROM THE FORGE AISLE — FORGE is the cold-aisle walk-through of a ROW; this is ONE RACK on the bench. They are TWO FEATURES, not two doors onto one, so the law is not engaged. That is John's call and it is his to make. (b) The GL objection is answered by machinery that ALREADY EXISTS AND IS UNCHANGED — and I VERIFIED IT RATHER THAN TRUSTING THE HANDOFF'S CLAIM: the guards are SYMMETRIC IN BOTH DIRECTIONS. rackElevation_render3D disposes a live _forge3dActive (:31752, added .222) and forge3d_open disposes a live _reh3dActive (:31676). _reh3dActive bounds live contexts to exactly 1. A dual-GL crash has no path. EDIT 1 — re-emit the toggle + the 3D mount. ⭐ PLACEMENT WAS THE ONE REAL DECISION AND THE HANDOFF LEFT IT TO DEVICE-VERIFY ('if the toggle breaks the canvas/minimap flex split, emit it full-width BEFORE the container instead'). THE CSS DECIDES IT OUTRIGHT, NO GUESSING AND NO PHONE NEEDED: .rack-hybrid is `display:flex; align-items:stretch`, so a toggle emitted as its CHILD becomes a THIRD FLEX COLUMN and shreds the 85/15 canvas/minimap split. It is therefore emitted FULL-WIDTH BEFORE .rack-hybrid, where it spans the panel and disturbs no existing geometry. #reh3dMount rejoins as a sibling of #rehFlatWrap INSIDE #reh3dCanvasHost — safe while FLAT, because .reh-3d-mount is display:none until .rack-hybrid-canvas gains .is-3d, and #rehFlatWrap is display:contents, so the fitted flat elevation lays out exactly as it does today. EDIT 2 — restore the session pref in the rAF block, AFTER rackFlat_applyFit so a restored 3D view can never race the flat fit. ⚠️ NON-OBVIOUS AND LOAD-BEARING: reh3d_restore() ALSO SETS window._reh3dRack, which reh3d_setMode() READS. So the call is NOT optional when the pref is off — without it the FIRST TAP on 3D would render a rack of `undefined`. Calling it unconditionally (inside the rd gate) is the correct wiring, not a redundancy. EDIT 3 — the .236 retirement comment is ANNOTATED, NOT DELETED (per the handoff and the re-home law): the reversal is argued in place so the next reader sees both the retirement and why it was overturned. LOCKED / UNTOUCHED, exactly as the spec demands: rackElevation_render3D internals · the tap-a-tray raycast -> openRmDevice(dev) wiring · the orbit-drag threshold · the GL cross-dispose guards · FORGE (locked canon) · the legacy house. FLAT REMAINS THE INSTANT DEFAULT — 3D is a per-session opt-in (REH3D_PREF_KEY = 'phantom_rackview_3d', sessionStorage), never auto-entered on a cold open, and three.js lazy-loads only on the first 3D tap. ?legacy=1 BYTE-IDENTICAL BY CONSTRUCTION: every new emission sits inside `if (_rehRd)` (redesign_isOn()), the legacy else-branch emits its original line verbatim, and reh3d_setMode() is unreachable in the legacy house — there is no #reh3dCanvasHost for it to find. Gates: node --check 4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS · CSS brace balance 3856/3856 IDENTICAL (no CSS edits, as expected) · CRLF preserved (bare-LF 0) · three-stamp lockstep .253 -> .254. ⚠️ NOTED, NOT FIXED (pre-existing, .218-era, and NOT mine to drive-by): reh3d_setMode() opens with `if (!host) return;` — a silent return on a user-facing path, which is a hard-rule-#1 shape. It is UNREACHABLE in practice (the buttons and #reh3dCanvasHost are emitted in the same render pass, so the host cannot be missing when a button exists), but this rewire makes that code LIVE again after 18 stamps dormant. Flagged for a future hardening ship rather than silently patched inside a spec'd rewire. ⚠️ BATCH: opens at 4 (.251 MEDIA CONV + .252 q3400-ra/magenta + .253 slate + .254 reh3d). ALL FOUR DEVICE-VERIFIES ARE OWED AND UNWALKED. The OPEN BOARD's own §6 holds the one-unverified-ship rule and calls the 7-deep freeze 'Sunday's lesson'; John directed this ship explicitly with the verifies outstanding, and that is his call to make — recorded here so the trail is honest. DEVICE-VERIFY (John, iPhone): [A] Rack detail opens FLAT INSTANTLY, identical to today — no regression, no flash of 3D. [B] The FLAT|3D toggle is visible ABOVE the elevation and does NOT break the canvas/minimap split (this is the placement call — if it reads wrong above the container, it is a one-block move). [C] Tap 3D: three.js lazy-loads, the solo rack renders in its locked framing. [D] Tap a tray in 3D -> the flat device detail opens; an ORBIT DRAG does NOT trigger it. [E] Toggle back to FLAT -> GL disposed clean; then open FORGE -> NO dual-GL crash. Then the reverse: open FORGE first, come back, tap 3D -> still no crash (both directions are guarded; both are worth a tap). [F] Kill + relaunch the PWA -> the pref is PER-SESSION, so it must come back FLAT, not 3D. [G] ?legacy=1 -> toggle absent, rack detail unchanged. STILL OWED — JOHN'S RULING (unchanged by this ship): DFW02's empty-model row @c1:001:38 · heights for gpu-b40-02 (160), cpu-gp2-01 (90), cpu-gp2-08 (75), inf-med-01 (60), om2216-c14 (16), fs-media-converter-chassis (5), VAST DBox (50), net-ufm-05 · does the .252 magenta read too loud on a dense rack · does a racked slate MEDIA CONV read as a real device rather than an empty slot.
